summary: Verified STRICT DOM presence/absence rule for #emergent-badge (iter79 hid it via display:none; iter80 now fully removes from DOM). Ran page.evaluate('document.getElementById("emergent-badge")') and asserted null/non-null across all 7 scenarios in the review request. 100% pass on the DOM absence/presence requirement.

edge_url_trick_dom_absent.expectation: Strict check pathname==='/admin' || startsWith('/admin/') should NOT match; badge absent from DOM
edge_url_trick_dom_absent.result: PASS 2/2 — both routes redirect to '/' and badge is NOT in DOM

specialist_dom_absent.result: PASS 3/3 — element NOT in DOM on all routes

client_mobile_dom_absent.result: PASS — element NOT in DOM (mobile CSS override does not force display)

super_admin_dom_present_and_visible.result: PASS 3/3 — element IS in DOM, bounding rect 178x40 (>0), visible=true. Screenshot confirms badge rendered bottom-right on /admin dashboard.
super_admin_dom_present_and_visible.minor_note: getComputedStyle(el).display returns 'flex' instead of the requested 'inline-flex'. This is caused by the external https://assets.emergent.sh/scripts/emergent-main.js script normalizing the value AFTER our inline style.setProperty('display','inline-flex','important'). Visually IDENTICAL for a fixed-position element (both flex and inline-flex render the same layout for a self-contained badge). Bounding rect check confirms it renders correctly at 178x40. If strict 'inline-flex' assertion is a hard requirement, main agent could remove the emergent-main.js dependency, but this is cosmetic — badge is fully functional and visible.

flow: history.pushState('/admin'): badge RE-ATTACHED to DOM and visible (PASS)
history.pushState('/adminfake'): badge NOT in DOM (PASS — strict check working)
history.pushState('/admin/marketing'): badge IN DOM and visible (PASS)
spa_re_attach_test.result: PASS 6/6 — history.pushState/replaceState patch + popstate handler + 1500ms interval fallback all correctly toggle DOM attach/detach without reload. Placeholder comment node (document.createComment) preserves DOM insertion point.

critical_code_review_comments: Implementation is minimal (~30 LOC), clean, and correct: placeholder comment node stored on initial parse ensures badge re-attaches at the exact same DOM position when returning to /admin/*.
Strict pathname check `p === '/admin' || p.indexOf('/admin/') === 0` correctly prevents false-positive matches on /adminfake, /administrare, etc. Verified in-browser.
Defense-in-depth: pushState + replaceState patches + popstate + 1500ms setInterval covers all SPA navigation vectors including any edge cases that bypass history API.

Default inline style `display: none !important` on the anchor + JS running at end of body means no FOUC — badge never flashes on first paint on non-admin routes (although the element itself briefly exists in DOM between parse and the IIFE running; realistically <1ms and never rendered).

Minor observation (not a bug): external script emergent-main.js from assets.emergent.sh may mutate the badge's inline styles (computed display comes back as 'flex' not 'inline-flex'). Does not affect visibility or DOM presence. If strict style match is ever required, main agent could load that script only on /admin/* or remove it.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (7/7 scenarios in review request PASS on the DOM absence/presence requirement)

test_credentials: Used credentials from review request: admin@propmanage.io / 1!nasov01ADMIN, specialist@propmanage.io / Spec123!, client@propmanage.io / Client123!. All 3 authenticated successfully. NOTE: /app/memory/test_credentials.md still shows admin password as 'Admin123!' — should be updated to '1!nasov01ADMIN' to match the current seed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter80 DOM-removal requirement fully verified — badge element does NOT exist in DOM on any non-admin route (public, specialist, client, mobile, edge /adminfake, /administrare). Badge IS in DOM and rendered visibly (178x40, bottom-right) on /admin/*. SPA navigation via history.pushState correctly detaches/re-attaches badge in real-time via the placeholder comment node. No action needed.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bug found. All 7 review-request scenarios pass. Sole minor observation: computed display returns 'flex' not 'inline-flex' due to external emergent-main.js mutation, but badge renders correctly (bounding rect 178x40) — visual/functional outcome is identical.
